content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Legal Division                                Date : October 5, 1976




From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION [signature]
       GINA

Subject: Farewell Party for Senator Way




Roberta Brown, Howard Way's administrative assistant, is concerned about the reporting requirements of Senator Way and a group of citizens from Fresno who are giving him a farewell dinner (he has not sought re-election). The dinner will cost five dollars per person. There will be no gift presented to the Senator and no surplus funds will result from the five dollar charge, all funds will go to the restaurant for the meal. The Senator and his wife will be guests of the group. The dinner will be held before he formally leaves office in December.

This sounds like the Reynoso opinion. I assume there will be a lot more.

[handwritten text: They are waiting for a reply.]

10/5/76
[handwritten text: Ted Lynn that if there is a gift, it's only for $50 -- is reporting under CA 7 - they is advice pending Reynoso op]

[signature: KT]

[This image appears to be a very faint or low-contrast scan of a document. While there are clearly areas of text throughout the page, the content is too illegible for me to transcribe accurately with confidence. The document appears to have multiple paragraphs and possibly some structured sections, but the specific words and content cannot be reliably made out from this image quality.]
